mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals impending economic contraction, prompting risk-off positioning and compressing equity multiples, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC drops below -0.05 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in consumer credit expansion and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, which boosts corporate revenue growth expectations and lifts equity prices over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 45% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands profit margins through operating leverage, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns as earnings revisions turn upward over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when GDP growth exceeds 3.5% annualized, or the rolling 1-year correlation between GDP surprises and SPX returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost inflation and supports equity valuations over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns show no statistically significant difference between high and low INDPRO growth regimes, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5%, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to compress equity risk premiums, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Equity multiples expand despite rates exceeding 4.5%, or the forward return differential between triggered and non-triggered periods is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong employment growth and rising unemployment claims indicates structural labor market stress, which triggers earnings downgrades and negative equity returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or the cross-sectional predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production combined with rising core inflation signals supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when INDPRO falls and CPIAUCSL rises simultaneously, or the joint signal's information ratio falls below 0.2 over a 3-year walk-forward test.
